data.content: I'll start `dotnet watch test` which automatically re-runs your tests whenever you save a file change.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has a .NET 8 MSTest project. They want hot reload to iterate faster. I can set up `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests whenever files change.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in the background for them.

data.content: The watcher may not have picked up the change yet. Let me touch the file to trigger a rebuild.
data.reasoningText:
The watcher seems to not have detected the file change. It may need `ImplicitUsings` enabled, or I need to add `<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>` to the csproj. But actually, I already added `using System;` — the watcher may just not have picked up the change yet. Let me check if the file was actually saved, then maybe the watcher needs a nudge. Let me also check if the watcher is still looking at the old version. It seems like `dotnet watch` printed the same output twice...

Actually, looking at this more carefully, `dotnet watch` said "Fix the error to continue" which means it's waiting for a file change to trigger a rebuild. 

The file should have been modified by my edit, but the watcher might not have registered it yet — I'll verify the file was actually saved and try forcing a refresh if needed.

data.content: Let me restart the watcher to get a clean run with the fix applied.
data.reasoningText:
It seems like the watcher is re-printing the same error. But wait — this is the same output? Or is it new? The output looks identical. Let me check if the issue is that `ImplicitUsings` is not enabled, so `System` namespace isn't being implicitly included. But I already added `using System;` explicitly. Let me check if the issue is that `read_bash` is re-outputting the same buffered output. 

Actually, let me look more carefully. The error says line 25 has `ArgumentException`. But I added `using System;` at line 1, so `ArgumentException` should now be in scope. 

Maybe the watcher isn't picking up my changes, or `dotnet watch` is serving cached output. Let me try stopping the watcher and enabling implicit usings in the project file instead, then restart to see if that resolves it. I should also double-check what line 25 actually contains now since my edit shifted everything down.
